mnemonics: Backtracking Bunnies: Place Queens in a Row, Backtrack When Conflict
Subset Sum Sorrow: Add Items, Remove When Insufficient
keyPoints: The n-Queens problem is NP-complete and can be solved using backtracking.
The Subset-sum problem is NP-complete and can be solved using backtracking.
Branch-and-Bound method is an exact approach for the Knapsack problem.
Approximation algorithms are used for NP-complete problems when an exact solution is not feasible.
The trade-off between time and space complexity is essential when choosing an algorithm.
NP-complete problems are a benchmark for measuring the hardness of a problem.
